questions.text: What is the major reason for which Allah created the jinn and humans?
questions.explanation: Allah says in the Quran (Adh-Dhariyat, 56): 'And I (Allah) created not the jinn and humans except that they should worship Me (Alone).'

questions.text: How many types (categories) of Tawheed are there?
questions.explanation: There are three types of Tawheed: Ar-Rabubiyyah (Lordship), Al-Uluhiyyah (Worship), and Al-Asma' was-Sifat (Names and Attributes).

questions.text: What does the word 'Tawheed' mean in Arabic?
questions.explanation: Tawheed comes from the root 'Wahada' which means to unify or make single. It is singling out Allah in what is exclusively His.

questions.text: What is Tawheed Ar-Rabubiyyah (Lordship)?
options: Singling out Allah in His Names and Attributes
Singling out Allah in His actions: creation, provision, giving life and death
questions.correctAnswer: Singling out Allah in His actions: creation, provision, giving life and death
questions.explanation: Tawheed Ar-Rabubiyyah is to recognize that Allah alone is the Creator, Provider, the One who gives life and death, and the One who manages the universe.

questions.text: What is Tawheed Al-Uluhiyyah (Worship)?
options: Believing that Allah is the Creator
Directing all acts of worship to Allah alone
questions.correctAnswer: Directing all acts of worship to Allah alone
questions.explanation: Tawheed Al-Uluhiyyah means singling out Allah in worship: prayer, fasting, supplication, sacrifice, hope, fear, love, etc., must all be directed to Him alone.

questions.text: What is Tawheed Al-Asma' was-Sifat (Names and Attributes)?
options: Believing that Allah is the Lord of the universe
Describing and naming Allah as He described and named Himself without resemblance or negation
questions.correctAnswer: Describing and naming Allah as He described and named Himself without resemblance or negation
questions.explanation: This type of Tawheed means affirming the Names and Attributes that Allah gave Himself without denying, distorting, or comparing them to creation.

questions.text: Did the polytheists of Makkah believe in Tawheed Ar-Rabubiyyah (that Allah is the Creator)?
questions.explanation: The polytheists acknowledged that Allah is the Creator, Provider, and the One who manages affairs, but they associated other deities in worship.

questions.text: Is believing only in Tawheed Ar-Rabubiyyah (Lordship) sufficient to be a Muslim?
questions.explanation: The polytheists of Makkah believed in Allah's Lordship but were not considered Muslims because they did not worship Him exclusively.

questions.text: Which type of Tawheed was the main mission of all prophets?
questions.explanation: All prophets were sent to call people to worship Allah alone, as this is the foundation upon which all deeds are based.

questions.text: What is the relationship between Tawheed and acts of worship?
options: Tawheed has no relation to deeds
Tawheed is a condition for the validity of acts of worship
Acts of worship are more important than Tawheed
questions.correctAnswer: Tawheed is a condition for the validity of acts of worship
questions.explanation: Without Tawheed, acts of worship are not valid, just as prayer is not valid without ablution (Wudu).

questions.text: What does worship become when performed without Tawheed?
options: It is simply reduced in reward
questions.explanation: Worship without Tawheed becomes Shirk, nullifies all deeds, and leads to eternal Hellfire if the person dies without repenting.

questions.text: According to the verse in Al-An'am (v.82), who will have security and guidance?
options: Those who believe and do not mix their faith with injustice (Shirk)
Those who do many voluntary prayers
questions.correctAnswer: Those who believe and do not mix their faith with injustice (Shirk)
questions.explanation: Allah says that those who believe and do not commit injustice (Shirk) will have security in this life and the hereafter, and will be rightly guided.

questions.text: Why did the polytheists of Makkah worship idols according to their own argument?
options: To get closer to Allah through them
Because they did not believe in Allah
questions.correctAnswer: To get closer to Allah through them
questions.explanation: When the Prophet asked them why they worshipped other gods, they replied that they only worshipped them so they could bring them closer to Allah.

questions.text: In which verse does Allah affirm that He is closer to man than his jugular vein?
questions.explanation: Allah says (Qaf, v.16): 'And indeed We have created man, and We know what his own self whispers to him. And We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.'

questions.text: Is Allah physically close to us as mentioned in the verse about the jugular vein?
questions.explanation: Allah is above His Throne, but He is close to us by His knowledge. The closeness mentioned is a closeness of knowledge, not physical.

questions.text: What did the Prophet ﷺ tell Mu'adh when he sent him to Yemen?
options: Call them first to give Zakat
Call them first to singling out Allah (Tawheed)
Call them first to perform Hajj
Call them first to read the Quran
questions.correctAnswer: Call them first to singling out Allah (Tawheed)
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'You are going to People of the Book. Let the first thing you call them to be the singling out of Allah.'

questions.text: What is the greatest pillar among the five pillars of Islam?
questions.explanation: Tawheed (expressed in the Shahada) is the greatest pillar because without it, one cannot become Muslim and no deed is accepted.

questions.text: Is Tawheed the reason why Allah created the jinn and humans?
questions.explanation: Allah says: 'And I created not the jinn and humans except that they should worship Me.' The worship mentioned here is Tawheed.

questions.text: What is Shirk (polytheism) in relation to Tawheed?
questions.explanation: Shirk is the opposite of Tawheed Al-Uluhiyyah. If Tawheed is not fulfilled, its opposite (Shirk) takes its place.

questions.text: What does the hadith say about the one who says 'La Ilaha Illa Allah' with sincerity?
options: Allah has forbidden the Hellfire from burning him
He will have no more problems in life
He will be exempt from prayer
questions.correctAnswer: Allah has forbidden the Hellfire from burning him
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Allah has forbidden the Fire from burning whoever says 'La Ilaha Illa Allah' seeking the Face of Allah (with sincerity).'

questions.text: What supplication did Prophet Yunus (Jonah) make in the belly of the whale?
options: Rabbi inni lima anzalta ilayya min khayrin faqir
La ilaha illa Anta Subhanaka inni kuntu min adh-dhalimin
Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min al-hammi wal-hazan
questions.correctAnswer: La ilaha illa Anta Subhanaka inni kuntu min adh-dhalimin
questions.explanation: Yunus declared the oneness of Allah saying: 'There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.' Through this supplication, he was saved.

questions.text: According to Ibn Ul-Qayyim, Tawheed helps whom when facing life's hardships?
options: Its friends and enemies in this life, but only its friends in the hereafter
questions.correctAnswer: Its friends and enemies in this life, but only its friends in the hereafter
questions.explanation: Ibn Ul-Qayyim explains that Tawheed saves even the polytheists from hardships in this life, and saves the believers in both this life and the hereafter.

questions.text: Which verse proves Tawheed Ar-Rabubiyyah by showing that Allah owns creation and command?
options: 'Worship Allah and join none with Him in worship'
'Surely, His is the Creation and Commandment'
'To Allah belong the Most Beautiful Names'
questions.correctAnswer: 'Surely, His is the Creation and Commandment'
questions.explanation: This verse from Surah Al-A'raf (v.54) establishes that creation and command belong exclusively to Allah, proving His Lordship.

questions.text: Which verse proves Tawheed Al-Asma' was-Sifat by negating any resemblance between Allah and His creation?
options: 'And I created not the jinn and humans except that they should worship Me'
'There is nothing like Him, and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer'
'Worship Allah and join none with Him in worship'
'Say: Who provides for you from the sky and the earth?'
questions.correctAnswer: 'There is nothing like Him, and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer'
questions.explanation: This verse from Surah Ash-Shura (v.11) establishes that Allah is unique in His Attributes, without any resemblance to creation.

questions.text: Can one become a Muslim by believing in only two of the three types of Tawheed?
questions.explanation: All three types of Tawheed are obligatory. Whoever believes in only one or two types has not yet truly singled out Allah.

questions.text: What is the greatest injustice mentioned in the Quran?
questions.explanation: Allah says: 'Indeed, Shirk is a great injustice' (Luqman, v.13). Ibn Kathir explains that Shirk is the greatest oppression.

questions.text: What does the verse 'To Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth' prove?
questions.explanation: This verse proves Allah's Lordship over the universe, showing that He is the only Master and Owner of the heavens and the earth.

questions.text: Which surah contains the verse about the creation of jinn and humans for worship?
questions.explanation: Verse 56 of Surah Adh-Dhariyat says: 'And I created not the jinn and humans except that they should worship Me.'

questions.text: Were the divine books revealed for a reason other than Tawheed?
questions.explanation: No prophet was sent and no book was revealed except for calling to the oneness of Allah and His exclusive worship.

questions.text: How does Ibn Kathir define the meaning of the verse about security and guidance (Al-An'am, v.82)?
options: Those who devote worship to Allah alone and associate no partners with Him
questions.correctAnswer: Those who devote worship to Allah alone and associate no partners with Him
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir explains that it refers to those who purified their worship for Allah alone, without associating partners, who attain pure security.
